metadata.summary: Set up Ruff, mypy, django-stubs, and pre-commit hooks as a unified linting and type-checking gate for Django projects.

content.title: Django Linting and Static Analysis Stack
content.summary: Every Django project MUST have Ruff (linting + formatting), mypy + django-stubs (static type checking), and pre-commit hooks committed to the repo. This replaces the legacy Flake8/Black/isort trinity and gates every commit automatically.

why.p: Manual code review catches logical errors; tools catch mechanical mistakes faster and at commit time. Ruff is 50x faster than the chained Flake8/isort/Black pipeline and covers pyflakes, pycodestyle, isort, bugbear, and pyupgrade in one binary. Type hints via mypy + django-stubs catch attribute errors (e.g., accessing a None FK) before they reach production. Pre-commit hooks prevent bad code from entering the repo. CI coverage thresholds enforce test discipline over time.

The 2025-2026 Django quality stack has consolidated: Ruff replaces Flake8/isort/Black entirely. mypy with django-stubs provides type safety for ORM queries, views, and serializers. One config source (pyproject.toml) covers ruff, mypy, pytest, and coverage — no legacy setup.cfg.

list.item: New Django project — wire pre-commit + ruff + mypy + django-stubs + Sentry on day one.
CI gate setup — block PRs on ruff, mypy, security scan, coverage threshold.
Adding type hints incrementally to an existing Django project.
Onboarding new developers who need mechanical enforcement of conventions.
Any project that cannot afford the cost of production attribute errors.
Throwaway prototypes — full quality stack costs more than the prototype is worth; use ruff alone.
Codebases on Django older than 4.2 — django-stubs examples assume recent versions.
Legacy projects under feature freeze — ROI on adding quality tooling is low; reserve for rewrites.

section.name: ruff-config
section.p: Ruff replaces Flake8, Black, isort, and pyupgrade in one binary. Configure once in pyproject.toml. Key rule groups: E/W (pycodestyle), F (pyflakes), I (isort), B (bugbear), C4 (comprehensions), UP (pyupgrade), SIM (simplify), DJ (Django-specific), T20 (no print statements).

rule.statement: Every Django project MUST configure Ruff in pyproject.toml with at minimum: target-version matching the Python version, line-length=88 or 100, rule groups E/W/F/I/B/C4/UP/SIM/DJ, and migrations excluded from checks. Run ruff check . --fix and ruff format . before every commit via pre-commit hooks.
rule.rationale: Ruff enforces consistent code style mechanically so code review focuses on logic. The DJ rule group catches Django antipatterns (null=True on string fields, deprecated URL patterns, missing __str__). T20 prevents print() leaking into production. Excluding migrations avoids false positives on generated code.

example.title: Ruff configuration in pyproject.toml
example.code:
[tool.ruff] target-version = "py312" line-length = 88 exclude = [ ".git", ".mypy_cache", ".ruff_cache", ".venv", "__pycache__", "migrations", "node_modules", ]
[tool.ruff.lint] select = [ "E", # pycodestyle errors "W", # pycodestyle warnings "F", # Pyflakes "I", # isort "B", # flake8-bugbear "C4", # flake8-comprehensions "UP", # pyupgrade "ARG", # flake8-unused-arguments "SIM", # flake8-simplify "TCH", # flake8-type-checking "DJ", # flake8-django "T20", # no print() "S", # bandit security checks "RUF", # Ruff-specific rules ] ignore = [ "E501", # Line too long (handled by formatter) "B008", # Function calls in defaults (needed for DI) "B904", # Allow raise without from (common in Django) "PLR0913", # Too many arguments "PLR2004", # Magic value comparison ]
[tool.ruff.lint.per-file-ignores] "tests/**/*.py" = [ "ARG", # Unused arguments in tests (fixtures) "S101", # asserts in tests are fine ] "**/migrations/*.py" = [ "E501", # Long lines in migrations are acceptable ]
[tool.ruff.lint.isort] known-first-party = ["myproject", "apps"] known-third-party = ["django", "rest_framework"]
[tool.ruff.format] quote-style = "double" indent-style = "space"

section.name: mypy-config
section.p: mypy with django-stubs provides type safety for Django ORM operations, views, and management commands. The plugin resolves Django's metaclass magic so mypy can understand QuerySet[Model], HttpRequest, and other framework types. django-stubs requires django_settings_module to resolve settings-dependent types.

rule.statement: Django projects with mypy MUST install django-stubs and configure [tool.django-stubs] with django_settings_module in pyproject.toml. Run mypy with check_untyped_defs=true and warn_unused_ignores=true. Apply mypy --strict incrementally per module (add to [tool.mypy.files] list), not repo-wide from day one. Every # type: ignore MUST include a comment explaining why it is acceptable.
rule.rationale: mypy + django-stubs catches ORM attribute errors (missing FKs, wrong field types) before runtime. check_untyped_defs=true catches errors in untyped functions. warn_unused_ignores keeps the codebase clean as stubs improve. Incremental strict mode prevents the "everything is broken" problem when enabling mypy on a large codebase.

example.title: mypy + django-stubs configuration
example.code:
[tool.mypy] python_version = "3.12" plugins = ["mypy_django_plugin.main", "mypy_drf_plugin.main"] check_untyped_defs = true warn_unused_ignores = true disallow_untyped_defs = true disallow_incomplete_defs = true warn_return_any = true warn_redundant_casts = true show_error_codes = true show_column_numbers = true
# Per-module overrides [[tool.mypy.overrides]] module = "*.migrations.*" ignore_errors = true
[[tool.mypy.overrides]] module = [ "celery.*", "kombu.*", "redis.*", ] ignore_missing_imports = true
[tool.django-stubs] django_settings_module = "config.settings"

example.title: Type-hinted Django code using django-stubs

antipattern.description: Using # type: ignore without a reason comment, or scattering ignore comments everywhere because the stubs are incomplete.
antipattern.reason: Unexplained ignores accumulate and hide real errors. For DRF serializers (where stubs are incomplete), install djangorestframework-stubs and document the specific limitation in the comment.

section.name: pre-commit-config
section.p: Pre-commit hooks run ruff and mypy automatically on changed files at commit time. Developers cannot bypass them (without --no-verify, which is forbidden). The CI pipeline runs the full suite on every push to catch regressions.

rule.statement: Every Django project MUST have a .pre-commit-config.yaml committed to the repo running at minimum: ruff (lint + fix), ruff-format, and mypy on changed Python files. Django system checks (python manage.py check) MUST run as a local hook. pytest runs on push (pre-push stage), not on commit — commit should complete in under 10 seconds. Use pre-commit install --install-hooks to ensure all developers have hooks installed.
rule.rationale: Pre-commit gates prevent broken code entering the repo without requiring discipline. Ruff + format on commit takes under 1 second. Django system checks catch misconfigured settings. Running pytest on push (not commit) prevents the 60-second-per-commit cost that causes developers to add --no-verify. CI is the second line of defense — it catches what pre-commit missed on the full diff.

example.title: pre-commit configuration
example.code:
# .pre-commit-config.yaml default_language_version: python: python3.12
repos: # Ruff - linting and formatting - repo: https://github.com/astral-sh/ruff-pre-commit rev: v0.9.2 hooks: - id: ruff args: [--fix, --exit-non-zero-on-fix] - id: ruff-format
# mypy - type checking - repo: https://github.com/pre-commit/mirrors-mypy rev: v1.14.1 hooks: - id: mypy additional_dependencies: - django-stubs[compatible-mypy] - djangorestframework-stubs[compatible-mypy] - types-requests args: [--config-file=pyproject.toml]
# Security checks via bandit - repo: https://github.com/PyCQA/bandit rev: 1.8.2 hooks: - id: bandit args: [-c, pyproject.toml] additional_dependencies: ["bandit[toml]"]
# Django system checks - repo: local hooks: - id: django-check name: Django system checks entry: python manage.py check language: system types: [python] pass_filenames: false
# pytest — on push only (not on every commit) - repo: local hooks: - id: pytest name: pytest entry: pytest --tb=short -q language: system types: [python] pass_filenames: false stages: [pre-push]

section.name: ci-quality-gate
section.p: The pre-commit hooks protect the local commit. CI enforces the full quality gate on every pull request and push, including coverage thresholds and migration checks. All tools configured in pyproject.toml — one file, no legacy setup.cfg.

rule.statement: CI pipelines for Django MUST run: (1) ruff check . --no-fix; (2) ruff format --check .; (3) mypy --strict apps/; (4) python manage.py check --deploy --fail-level WARNING; (5) python manage.py makemigrations --check --dry-run; (6) pip-audit --strict; (7) pytest -n auto --cov=apps --cov-fail-under=80. These MUST run on every pull_request and push to main. Coverage threshold of 80% MUST be enforced as a blocking gate.
rule.rationale: CI is the authoritative gate. Pre-commit runs on changed files only; CI runs on the full diff. --deploy flag catches production-only misconfigurations. makemigrations --check catches engineers who changed models without generating migrations. pip-audit blocks deployment of vulnerable dependencies. Coverage threshold prevents test debt accumulation.

example.title: CI quality gate script
example.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash # scripts/quality-gate.sh — Django pre-deploy quality gate. set -euo pipefail export DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE="${DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE:-config.settings.production}"
echo "==> ruff (lint + format check)" ruff check . --no-fix ruff format --check .
echo "==> mypy --strict on apps/" mypy --strict apps/
echo "==> Django deploy checks" python manage.py check --deploy --fail-level WARNING
echo "==> Missing migrations?" python manage.py makemigrations --check --dry-run
echo "==> Dependency CVE scan" pip-audit --strict || { echo "FAIL: pip-audit found vulnerable deps"; exit 1; }
echo "==> Tests + coverage" pytest -n auto --cov=apps --cov=core --cov-fail-under=80 --cov-branch
echo "==> Quality gate passed."

example.title: pytest + coverage configuration
example.code:
[tool.pytest.ini_options] DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE = "config.settings.test" python_files = ["test_*.py", "*_test.py"] addopts = [ "--strict-markers", "--strict-config", "-ra", "--tb=short", "--cov=apps", "--cov-report=term-missing:skip-covered", "--cov-report=html", "--cov-fail-under=80", ] filterwarnings = [ "error", "ignore::DeprecationWarning", "ignore::django.utils.deprecation.RemovedInDjango60Warning", ] markers = [ "slow: marks tests as slow", "integration: marks tests as integration tests", ]
[tool.coverage.run] source = ["apps"] branch = true omit = [ "*/migrations/*", "*/tests/*", "*/__init__.py", "*/admin.py", ]
[tool.coverage.report] exclude_lines = [ "pragma: no cover", "def __repr__", "raise AssertionError", "raise NotImplementedError", "if __name__ == .__main__.:", "if TYPE_CHECKING:", "if settings.DEBUG:", "@abstractmethod", ] fail_under = 80 show_missing = true skip_covered = true

section.name: gotchas
section.p: Common agent pitfalls when setting up the Django linting stack.

antipattern.description: Running mypy repo-wide with --strict from day one on an existing codebase.
antipattern.reason: Hundreds of errors appear immediately and developers disable mypy entirely. Enable strict per-module in [tool.mypy.overrides] incrementally, allowing the team to fix one module at a time.
The plugin cannot resolve settings-dependent types (INSTALLED_APPS, AUTH_USER_MODEL) without the settings module path. Results in spurious errors across the entire ORM layer.

antipattern.description: Adding T20 (no-print) rule to ruff but leaving print() calls in management commands and scripts.
antipattern.reason: Management commands legitimately use self.stdout.write(), not print(). Add T20 per-file-ignore for management/commands/ or convert to self.stdout.write().

antipattern.description: Running MegaLinter or full linting suite as a pre-commit hook instead of CI-only.
antipattern.reason: Heavy containers add 30+ seconds per commit; developers bypass with --no-verify and the local gate dies. Native tools (ruff, mypy) complete in under 1 second per commit.

section.name: checklist
section.p: Pre-commit setup checklist for a new Django project.

checklist.check: Ruff configured in pyproject.toml with E/W/F/I/B/C4/UP/SIM/DJ/T20/S rule groups
mypy configured with django-stubs plugin and django_settings_module
pre-commit-config.yaml committed with ruff, ruff-format, mypy, django-check hooks
pre-commit install --install-hooks run in project setup docs
CI workflow runs ruff check --no-fix, ruff format --check, mypy, check --deploy
CI runs makemigrations --check --dry-run to catch missing migrations
pytest coverage threshold set to 80% and enforced as blocking gate
pyproject.toml is the single config source (no setup.cfg, no .flake8, no .isort.cfg)
